TextView.android:text: Privacy-first defaults. Transcript storage is OFF by default.
Unlock premium features and help fund new smartglasses support.
Local Models run fully on-device with GGUF files. Tasker mode uses your Tasker/AutoInput profile.
Automatically captures text from your screen for AI memory. Use blacklist for sensitive apps (banking, password managers).
Get a daily notification to review and confirm facts about your day for AI memory.
If enough time has passed, regeneration is queued in background and won't block chat replies.
Customize prompts used by local models to generate daily fact bullets from screen captures.

com.google.android.material.switchmateri.android:text: Extract user fact candidates from chats

TextView.android:text: Personalize how the AI agent behaves and what it knows about you.
When ON, the app will start audio recording on the glasses, stop after 15 minutes, then start a new recording repeatedly.
This feature is under active development. Use with caution.
Limits how many UI actions the agent can perform per request.
When OFF, the app should store only summaries + minimal metadata.
When OFF, exports should omit full transcript (best-effort).
Uses simple heuristics (capitalized names, emails, phone numbers).
Delete all local chats, notes, capture sessions, and recordings on this device.
Agent runtime controls have been moved to the LOCAL AGENT section above for better organization.
This section provides the LocalAgentService intent API for external integrations.
How do I set Local Models?
Select Local Models in AI / Automation, then tap Configure Local Models and follow the setup steps on device.
Do I need a subscription to use the app?
No. You can use Tasker or Local Models without subscribing. Pro is optional and adds premium managed features.
By default data is stored locally on your phone. You can export/import your local data and clear it any time from the Data section.
Can I review the source code?
Yes. The app is open source and you can manually review the full source code on GitHub.
